reasoning: While GoPro faces declining revenues and operating losses, its debt maturity wall (the November 2025 Notes) falls several months after the July 1, 2025 cutoff. As of the end of 2024, GoPro maintained $102.8 million in cash, which is sufficient to cover the remaining $93.8 million in notes principal if they choose to pay them down rather than refinance. The company has also implemented significant cost-cutting measures (30% reduction in op-ex) and there were no credible reports of a bankruptcy filing or 'going concern' warning in their March 2025 Form 10-K. A widely circulated news report from March 2025 about a 'popular outdoor brand' warning of bankruptcy was identified as referring to Solo Brands (Solo Stove), not GoPro. Consequently, an imminent bankruptcy filing before July 1 is highly unlikely.
